When people turn away from serving Satan and friendship with the world, to make peace with God and join Israel, they should not be surprised if the world hates them, and their former friends become enemies. By such methods Satan discourages many who are convinced of their danger and almost persuaded to become Christians, but fear the cross. These things should encourage us to ask God for protection, help, and deliverance.

The weakest and most feeble people, who have just begun to trust the Lord, are as much entitled to be protected as those who have long and faithfully served Him. It is our duty to defend the afflicted, who, like the Gibeonites, are brought into trouble on our account or for the sake of the gospel. Joshua would not forsake his new vassals. How much less will our true Joshua fail those who trust in Him! We may be lacking in our trust, but our trust never can lack success. Yet God’s promises are not to slow down and disappear, but to quicken and encourage our efforts. Notice the great faith of Joshua, and the power of God answering it by miraculously stopping the sun, so that the day of Israel's victories could be made longer. Joshua acted on this occasion by impulse from his mind from the Spirit of God. It was not necessary for Joshua to speak or for the miracle to be recorded, according to modern terms of astronomy. The sun appeared to the Israelites over Gibeon, and the moon over the valley of Ajalon, and there they appeared to be stopped on their course for one whole day. Is anything too hard for the Lord? is a sufficient answer to ten thousand difficulties that objectors have raised in every age against the truth of God as revealed in His written word. Proclamation was made by which the neighboring nations were told: Behold the works of the Lord, and say, What nation is there so great as Israel, who has God so near unto them?

None spoke against any of the children of Israel. This shows their perfect safety. The kings were called to account as rebels against Israel. Places of refuge based on lies will only secure God’s judgment. God punished the abominable wickedness of these kings, whose measure of iniquity was now full. And by this public act of justice done upon these ringleaders of the Canaanites in sin, He would fill His people with greater dread and detestation of the sins of the nations that God cast out from before them. Here is a type and figure of Christ's victories over the powers of darkness, and of believers’ victories through Him. In our spiritual conflicts we must not be satisfied with obtaining some important victory. We must pursue our scattered enemies, searching out the remains of sin as they rise up in our hearts, and thus pursue the conquest. In so doing, the Lord will afford light until the warfare is accomplished.

Joshua made haste in taking these cities. See what a great deal of work may be done in a little time if we are diligent and improve our opportunities. God here showed His hatred of the idolatries and other abominations of which the Canaanites had been guilty, and shows us how great the provocation was by the greatness of the destruction brought upon them. Here also was typified the destruction of all the enemies of the Lord Jesus, who, having rejected the riches of His grace, must forever feel the weight of His wrath. The Lord fought for Israel. They could not have gotten victory if God had not undertaken the battle. We conquer when God fights for us; if He is for us, who can be against us?
